Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Seems reasonable. It would be useful to have some kind of simple test, that sets a couple of permanent parameters, then dumps them and verifies they are present and have the same value.

The next logical patch would be to allow lctl set_param and conf_param to accept the YAML input to restore the parameters.

Patch Set 1:

> Seems reasonable. It would be useful to have some kind of simple
 > test, that sets a couple of permanent parameters, then dumps them
 > and verifies they are present and have the same value.
 > 
 > The next logical patch would be to allow lctl set_param and
 > conf_param to accept the YAML input to restore the parameters.

Agreed, that's the next logical patch.  I'll work on a test as well.

Patch Set 4:

Sorry my comment was confusing. What I meant is that you do

char *tmp = strchr(lustre_cfg_string(lcfg, 1), '=');

tmp is directly pointing to an offset in the lcfg buffer. So when you do *tmp = '\0' you are altering a lcfg buffer itself. That is not safe. A better that doesn't involve altering the lcfg buffer is to use snprintf %.*s and print only a specific length of the lcfg buffer which is what you are trying to do.

Patch Set 4:

> Sorry my comment was confusing. What I meant is that you do
 > 
 > char *tmp = strchr(lustre_cfg_string(lcfg, 1), '=');
 > 
 > tmp is directly pointing to an offset in the lcfg buffer. So when
 > you do *tmp = '\0' you are altering a lcfg buffer itself. That is
 > not safe. A better that doesn't involve altering the lcfg buffer is
 > to use snprintf %.*s and print only a specific length of the lcfg
 > buffer which is what you are trying to do.

Got it, will fix.
